title: Lachnagrostis perennis
intro: Lachnagrostis perennis, also known as blue oat grass, is a perennial grass species native to Australia and New Zealand. The health benefits of blue oat grass include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve digestion, due to its rich content of bioactive compounds. Bioactive constituents of Lachnagrostis perennis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from blue oat grass include teas, infusions, and extracts, often used to support digestive health and reduce symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
taxonomy: Lachnagrostis perennis is classified as a perennial grass species. It belongs to the family Poaceae.
